title: docker and libpod endpoint of Rest API V2
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


/kind feature

**Description**

The Post that announces the new rest api v2 https://podman.io/blogs/2020/01/17/podman-new-api.html describes that there are 2 main entpoints.

one For docker and one for libpos

```
<endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
And if you wanted a list of images but in docker-compatibility, you would use:
<endpoint_base_url>/images/json
```

wouldn't it be better to have libpod and docker in  separate

```
<endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
<endpoint_base_url>/docker/images/json
```

comments.body:
I don't think we have support for TCP listening in the service command yet,
only unix socket. It is desperately in need of documentation, though

On Mon, Jan 27, 2020, 11:13 Stefan Bischof <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> *Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)*
>
> /kind feature
>
> *Description*
>
> The Post that announces the new rest api v2
> https://podman.io/blogs/2020/01/17/podman-new-api.html describes that
> there are 2 main entpoints.
>
> one For docker and one for libpos
>
> <endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
> And if you wanted a list of images but in docker-compatibility, you would use:
> <endpoint_base_url>/images/json
>
> wouldn't it be better to have libpod and docker in separate
>
> <endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
> <endpoint_base_url>/docker/images/json
>
> ------------------------------
>
> just to verify ( i know that it is not released so not a bug)
>
> if i run podman service -t 50000 tcp:localhost:8881 (without sudo)
> i got
> Error: could not get runtime: eventer creation: No support for journald
> logging
>
> with sudo podman service -t 50000 tcp:localhost:8881
> it starts but in only got a 404 -Not Found calling
> http://localhost:8881/libpod/images/json
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4990?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCH5X3Y33CSWZ57CO7DQ72XVHA5CNFSM4KL6USK2YY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFUVEXG43VMWVGG33NNVSW45C7NFSM4II33QXA>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCHPGWFXH5MQZOD7FRLQ72XVHANCNFSM4KL6USKQ>
> .
>

The Main Question is about the fact that the libpod endpoints `/libpod` are inside the Docker endpoint `/` paths


-------

i will remove my question about tcp:/ because in general this will work. an i wait until the tests are commited https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4894

@mheon 

the docs showed me the way to tcp:/

´For example, *unix://tmp/foobar.sock* or *tcp:localhost:8080*.´

and answer with a `404` sounds like http

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/aa13779e9e8d5bb6090d514e3aad9387cb0a2d35/docs/source/markdown/podman-service.1.md#L8-L15

this also works!!!
`sudo podman service -t 50000 tcp:localhost:8881`
`curl -s -X GET -H 'Content-type: application/json' localhost:8881/_ping`

the service command supports a uri format of tcp or unix more or less.  the format of the URI will change slightly when I get a chance to correct it (tcp:/ vs tcp:// vc tcp:).  I believe the issue you hit is that you don't have the version in the path "v1.24"

All I did with tcp / http / rest is just to test the new features:

This ticket is because i think that the /libpod endpoint should be beside not inside the docker one

```
<endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
<endpoint_base_url>/docker/images/json
```

not

```
<endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
<endpoint_base_url>/images/json
```

The installed base of "docker" api callers are going to call
<endpoint_base_url>/images/json, this is not something we can chage.
<endpoint_base_url>/libpod/images/json
is not under
<endpoint_base_url>/images/json
